channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Labor upgrades migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget
item.description: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: International students are the most common temporary migrants arriving in Australia.

item.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave Penrith is as unprecedented as his success
item.description: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Cleary will coach Penrith until the end of next season.

item.title: US author to be sentenced for killing husband then writing a book on grief
item.description: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

media:group.media:description: Kouri Richins is set to be sentenced for her husband's murder after writing a children's book about grief.

item.title: Miner to sue council for $22m in years-long dispute over access road
item.description: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Liam O'Connell

media:group.media:description: David Luke's house at Mooray Station is close to the road used by heavy vehicles to access the mine.

item.title: John Travolta's luxury jet bound for aviation museum arrives by ship
item.description: After nearly a decade of delays and millions of dollars in repairs, John Travolta's old luxury jet needed a boat to get to Australia.
item.dc:creator: Justin Huntsdale

media:group.media:description: Hollywood star John Travolta talks to media in front of the Connie aircraft at the Shellharbour Airport in November 2019.

item.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding
item.description: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall and Arj Ganesan

media:group.media:description: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks in March 2025.

item.title: Woman stole parrot from pet shop by hiding it under top, police allege
item.description: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

media:group.media:description: SA Police say the bird, a turquoise green-cheeked conure, was from a Melrose Park pet store.

item.title: $150m compensation payout for Yindjibarndi slammed as too little
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.
item.dc:creator: Alistair Bates and Mietta Adams

media:group.media:description: Michael Woodley speaks after Yindjibarndi was awarded $150.1 million in compensation from Andrew Forrest's Fortescue.

item.title: Forget the generation gap, this budget is about 'the 1 per cent' and the rest
item.description: Just like it did in 2019, Labor is making an argument about wealth in Australia when it proposes negative gearing and capital gains tax changes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers says his tax changes are the most substantial in a generation.

item.title: Ex-Demon May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt AFL retirement
item.description: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

media:group.media:description: Steven May says a mental breakdown on the verge of the season led to his abrupt AFL retirement in March.

item.title: Person shot at property in Tasmania's north
item.description: Tasmania Police say a person has been shot in the stomach at a residential property at Wynyard, in the state's north.

media:group.media:description: Tasmania Police drone operators at Mount Hicks Road, as part of the investigation.

item.title: Could cars save this struggling country mall?
item.description: A Victorian council is proposing to reopen a regional mall to vehicles after 44 years in an attempt to breathe new life into the area.
item.dc:creator: Tyrone Dalton, Anna McGuinness, and Charlotte Wilkes

media:group.media:description: Hargreaves Mall has a vacancy rate of 31 per cent and has struggled with shop closures for decades.

item.title: Police searching for man after woman's body found outside cafe
item.description: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup.
item.dc:creator: Greig Johnston

media:group.media:description: A note on the cafe window where the woman's body was found.

item.title: Qld ministerial lovers and premier referred to CCC by opposition
item.description: The call for investigation follows weeks of scrutiny over whether ministers Amanda Camm and Tim Mander had made appropriate conflict of interest declarations relating to their relationship.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster

media:group.media:description: Tim Mander and Amanda Camm's relationship has been scrutinised in recent weeks.

item.title: Omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA to prevent preterm birth
item.description: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Bonnie Haselgrove took part in the trial while pregnant with Evan who is now six months old.

item.title: Inquest finds treatment of prisoner in lead up to death 'reprehensible'
item.description: Corey Desmond Devree died when he was left alone for extended periods of time at the prison's paint workshop.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Becerra Mellet and Madigan Landry

media:group.media:description: Corey Desmond Devree died by suicide while incarcerated at Bunbury Regional Prison in October 2024.

item.title: Ex-bikie boss who paid sniper to kill rival gets 34 years in jail
item.description: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.
item.dc:creator: David Weber

media:group.media:description: David Pye, pictured here outside court in 2013.

item.title: Inquiry witness claims CFMEU planted asbestos to get project shut down
item.description: The inquiry heard the CFMEU was making social media posts attacking the credibility of WHSQ employees.
item.dc:creator: Tobi Loftus

media:group.media:description: The CFMEU inquiry heard asbestos was allegedly planted at the Cairns Performing Arts Centre construction site.

item.title: Homebuilding reforms to let WA customers walk away from failing builders sooner
item.description: New legislation introduced to state parliament aim to prevent Western Australians from having to suffer the financial and emotional strain of not having a home if their building company collapses.
item.dc:creator: Cason Ho

media:group.media:description: Nelson and Kirstyn Pray inspecting their unfinished Nicheliving home in Orelia.

item.title: Ship owner bid to limit payout over tugboats sinking fails
item.description: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.
item.dc:creator: Sandy Powell

media:group.media:description: TasPorts is trying to sue the owner of the Goliath for $22 million in compensation to cover the salvage, clean up and damage.

item.title: NT Police fraud squad seizes grenades, warheads in raids south of Darwin
item.description: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

media:group.media:description: Police seized 32 explosive ordnance items suspected of having military origin, including munitions, hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids on properties south of Darwin last month.

item.title: Budget's $600m for Bondi attack response branded 'quite modest'
item.description: The Executive Council of Australian Jewry has welcomed a $600 million federal budget funding commitment for the Jewish community, but says it is a "small comfort" for those who lost loved ones in the Bondi terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Corvo, Rhiannon Soliman-Marron, and Jessica Kidd

media:group.media:description: The federal budget includes more than $600 million to support Australia's Jewish community after the Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist advised to test for bloodborne viruses
item.description: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

media:group.media:description: Patients may have been exposed to HIV, hepatitis B, or hepatitis C and are advised to get tested as a precaution.

item.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
item.description: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Ivan and Nathan Cleary after winning their fourth straight premiership in 2024.

item.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
item.description: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.
item.dc:creator: Lewis Wiseman

media:group.media:description: There are several parts of the latest federal budget that will have an impact on older Australians.
